title: oxytropis leucantha subsp. leucantha
intro: Oxytropis leucantha subsp. leucantha, also known as the White Oxytropis or White Milkvetch, is a perennial legume plant native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Oxytropis leucantha subsp. leucantha include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, as well as its use as a treatment for various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for internal use, and it can also be used topically as a poultice or salve to treat skin conditions.
